title: Jianxiang Tan
text:
我的 个人主页 是基于 Chi Zhang (github) 的个人主页修改而来的。
你可以 Fork 本仓库，然后酌情修改你的个人信息，编译后即可生成你的个人主页。这种方法无需注册域名，也不用学习 HTML 和 CSS 等前端知识，直接使用 GitHub Pages 即可完成个人主页的搭建。
具体制作过程参见：

连小白, 2025, 50 分钟搞定个人主页：Fork 模板 + GitHub Pages + Quarto 完整教程.

本仓库的使用方法：

你可以点击本页右上方的 ⭐Star 按钮来收藏本仓库，方便日后查阅；
也可以点击 Use this template 按钮来快速 Fork 本仓库。
你制作个人主页期间有任何问题，可以在 Discussions 提问和讨论。



主页 | 课程 | 视频 | 推文 | 资料

title: Conferences
text:
2026

Apr — IRSPM2026 (Edith Cowan University, Australia)
Yanto Chandra, Jianxiang Tan — “Will Future Employees Prefer ‘AI as Street-level Managers’ in the Workplace?”
Feb — PUBSIC2026 (Corvinus University of Budapest, Hungary)
Jianxiang Tan, Guanru Zhang, Helen Bao, & Yanto Chandra — “Public Blame in Algorithmic Welfare Services: Citizens’ Allocation of Responsibility Across the Network of Actors.”



2025

Jun — PMRC2025 (Seoul National University, Korea)
Yanto Chandra & Jianxiang Tan — “Mapping the Landscape of Public Sector Innovation: A Topic Modeling Approach.”
Apr — IRSPM2025 (University of Bologna, Italy)
Jianxiang Tan & Yanto Chandra — “Artificial Intelligence Recommendations and Citizen Compliance: A Public Value Creation Perspective.”
Yanto Chandra, Yiyun Luo, & Jianxiang Tan — “Trust Me, I am Almost a Doctor: Uncertainty in AI-mediated Doctor-Patient Interactions.”
Yanto Chandra & Jianxiang Tan — “Probing the Past to Chart Plausible Futures in Public Sector Innovation: Toward a Design Science in AI-Driven Public Administration.”



2024

Nov — AI & Public Policy International Conference (Nanyang Technological University, Singapore)
Jianxiang Tan & Yanto Chandra — “Artificial Intelligence Recommendations and Citizen Compliance: A Public Value Creation Perspective.”
Yanto Chandra & Jianxiang Tan — “Predicting Public Service Innovation Outcomes using Generative Artificial Intelligence.”

title: Jianxiang Tan
text:
jianxitan3-c@my.cityu.edu.hk
  
  
    
     Jianxiang Tan
  

  
  

Jianxiang Tan is currently a PhD candidate at the City University of Hong Kong. His research focuses on designing governance frameworks for artificial intelligence applications in the public sector, exploring how to balance technological efficacy with public interests, with particular emphasis on pathways to realize public values such as transparency, responsiveness, and accountability.





Oct 2025 – Mar 2026: Visiting Scholar (PhD student), University of Cambridge


Sep 2023 – Present: PhD candidate, City University of Hong Kong


Sep 2020 – Jul 2023: Master, Wuhan University


Sep 2016 – Jul 2020: Bachelor, Nankai University






AI governance
Public sector innovation
Public values





Research Tuition Scholarship, City University of Hong Kong (2025)
